rkagi 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • rkagi Quickstart Guide

Perform a Kagi search in one call

kagi_search_once.Rd

This is a high-level convenience wrapper around new_kagi_connection(), new_kagi_search(), and kagi_perform(). It constructs a connection, prepares a search request, and performs it in a single step.

Usage

kagi_search_once(
  q,
  limit = NULL,
  api_key = Sys.getenv("KAGI_API_KEY"),
  base_url = "https://kagi.com/api/v0",
  path = NULL
)

Arguments

q

Character scalar. The search query string. This can include search operators such as filetype:pdf, site:example.com, boolean logic (AND, OR), quoted phrases, etc.

limit

Optional integer. Maximum number of results to request. If NULL (default), the API will return its own default number of hits.

api_key

API key to use for authentication. By default this is read from the KAGI_API_KEY environment variable. For best practice, set Sys.setenv(KAGI_API_KEY="...") or add it to your ~/.Renviron. Advanced users can also pass a function that resolves the key lazily (see new_kagi_connection()).

base_url

Base URL for the Kagi API. Defaults to "https://kagi.com/api/v0". Override only if you are testing against a mock server or a future API version.

path

Optional file path. If supplied, the raw JSON response will be written to this path for reproducibility or debugging.

Value

An object of class kagi_search_results. Use helper functions to extract components:

kagi_hits()

Return a tibble of primary search hits.

kagi_related()

Character vector of related queries.

kagi_meta()

List of response metadata (latency, node, API balance).

See also

new_kagi_connection(), new_kagi_search(), kagi_perform.kagi_search(), kagi_hits(), kagi_related(), kagi_meta()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Simple one-liner
res <- kagi_search_once("openalex api", limit = 3)
kagi_hits(res)

# With a complex query
q <- build_kagi_q(query = "biodiversity",
                  filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),
                  site = c("example.com", "gov"))
res <- kagi_search_once(q, limit = 5)
kagi_hits(res)
} # }
